event_blocks.summary: A vintage warning screen and YouTube Originals logo precede a title card for 'BROCKTON, MASS.' as a distorted rock instrumental begins. A Young Man on Bicycle, wearing a dark jacket and blue jeans, rides across a weathered metal bridge and down snowy stairs, continuing through a quiet, run-down urban neighborhood. Approaching a street corner, the music fades, replaced by distant traffic sounds, where he encounters Three Young Men near a Dilapidated Blue Building with peeling paint and a faded 'BROCKTON' sign. The cyclist stops, dismounts, and faces them; one of the Three Young Men smokes a cigarette. A male voice asks, "What?", followed by, "Screw off, you fucking freak." A dog barks distantly, and the commanding voice reiterates, "I said go." The Young Man on Bicycle then aggressively shouts, "Get the fuck out of here!", initiating a physical altercation with sounds of struggle, impacts, and shattering glass. Amidst the chaos, one of the Three Young Men exclaims, "What the fuck?", while the Young Man on Bicycle retorts, "You asshole!" and demands, "Hey! Who did that, huh? Who the fuck did that?! You? Break my window, you bitch? Break my window? Huh? Punch it hits me, you little bitch! Hit me!" The fight escalates with punches and grunts, and one of the Three Young Men yells, "Yeah!" The Young Man on Bicycle is overwhelmed, expressing "What the fuck?" before being knocked to the ground with heavy impacts and grunts, landing in the snow with blood on his face. The Three Young Men stand over him, some shocked, others aggressive. The injured young man stumbles forward, clutching his side, as aggressive shouts of "Get out of here!" and "I'll fucking kill you!" are heard. One of the Three Young Men, wearing a plaid shirt, reacts with shock. The injured young man wipes blood from his mouth and walks away down a cracked street, the sounds of a garbage truck passing audibly in the background. One of the Three Young Men exclaims, "Man! What the fuck?" A distant train horn sounds before a title card sequence reads 'WAYNE', 'CHAPTER ONE', and 'GET SOME THEN...', accompanied by a low, driving rock/metal music track. An aerial shot reveals a snow-covered neighborhood with train tracks, and the young man is seen descending a weathered metal bridge, holding a red object, his demeanor determined, as the intense music emphasizes the harsh setting.

What the fuck?'.

event_blocks.summary: In a snow-dusted urban neighborhood, a Young Man on Bicycle, bleeding from the face and clutching a red object, is confronted by an Older Man with Dog demanding rent for his father. Despite the Young Man's explanation that his father is sick, the Older Man insists on payment, then violently tackles the Young Man. The Young Man escapes into the dimly lit interior of House Number 328, a dilapidated apartment, pursued by the Older Man's threats. Later, the Young Man exits the house and startles a Young Woman on the porch who is selling cookies. He apologizes for his father's interrupted sleep, explaining his father has cancer. The Young Woman, initially suspicious, eventually accepts his offer to come inside after he assures her he has no inappropriate intentions, recalling a past uncomfortable encounter. The bleak surroundings of bare trees and snow underscore the tense, isolated atmosphere.

raw_segments: The scene opens in a desolate, snow-dusted urban neighborhood, where a Young Man on Bicycle, visibly injured with blood on his face and clutching a red object, walks away from the camera. He is immediately confronted by an Older Man with Dog, who aggressively calls out, "Boy! Come here! Hey, you hear me, boy? I want to talk to you." The Young Man, wearing a dark jacket and blue jeans, responds defensively, "I'm eating my lunch." The Older Man, in a black leather jacket, demands, "Your father is not answering the door. I need my rent, that son of a bitch is ducking me." The Young Man explains, "No, he can't answer, he's sick," but the Older Man dismisses this, stating, "I don't give two shits. Sick or not, he's still got to pay his rent." After a brief pause, the Older Man calls out "Hey!" and the Young Man, resigned, says, "I'll get it." The Older Man mutters about the Young Man's father, then suddenly lunges and tackles the Young Man to the ground in a violent shove. The Young Man screams in pain, "Ah! Son of a bitch! You fucker, you did that on purpose, you asshole! Ow!" as he quickly gets up and runs towards a white brick house numbered 328. The Older Man, enraged, shouts, "Where the fuck you going, you fucker? Get back here, you son of a bitch!" The Young Man defiantly yells "Fuck you!" while fleeing. The Older Man continues to threaten, "Get back here! I'm not done with you! I want my money, you bastard!" The Young Man, displaying a pained yet determined expression, enters the dimly lit, run-down interior of the apartment, still gasping and coughing from the altercation, before walking deeper into the cluttered space past a small table and bed. The atmosphere is heavy with tension and the aftermath of violence.

The scene opens with the Young Man, previously on a bicycle, now standing in a dimly lit interior, his expression tense and weary. He abruptly exits the white brick house at address '328', stepping onto its weathered wooden porch, startling a Young Woman who is crouched by a red backpack, organizing items. He exclaims "What!" followed by a "Shit!" as she responds with a sharp "What? What?" and then, "I'm trying to sell you some goddamn cookies is what!" The Young Man, wearing a dark jacket over a gray and yellow striped hoodie, apologizes, explaining, "Sorry, my my dad's sleeping." The Young Woman, in a maroon hoodie with a yellow and gray striped hood, patterned jeans, and white sneakers, her dark hair tied back, challenges him, "Well why the hell didn't you just say that then? Shoot." She then questions why his dad is sleeping in the middle of the day, asking if he's lazy. The Young Man reveals, "Nah, he's got cancer." She asks if people with cancer can still eat cookies, to which he replies his dad hasn't been eating much. She then turns her attention to him, asking if he likes cookies or is "as weird as you look," and he affirms, "I love cookies." She lists her cookie selection: peanut butter, coconut (which she describes as tasting like "dog shit"), and lemon. The Young Man, still observing her with a mix of concern and hesitation, eventually asks, "Uh, you wanna come in?" She responds cautiously, "You ain't gonna do anything weird, right?" When he asks "Weird like what?" she recounts a past encounter: "Like some guy down on tour, he asked if he could see my feet." He quickly assures her, "I don't wanna see your feet." She then says, "Yeah, whatever. It's fine, I guess," as she continues to adjust her backpack. The bleak urban environment, with bare trees, patches of snow, and distant modest houses, reinforces the tense, isolated atmosphere of their minimal but charged interaction.

event_blocks.summary: A young woman, identifiable by her maroon hoodie with a yellow and gray striped hood and a red backpack, cautiously ascends a dimly lit, narrow staircase, her footsteps echoing with creaking sounds. She enters a cluttered, dimly lit bedroom adorned with patterned wallpaper, posters for 'DEVIL'S NIGHT' and 'CHUCK YOURSELF,' a glowing green lava lamp, a leaning stop sign, and a stereo system beneath tattered curtains. Inside, a young man in a gray t-shirt and dark jeans is heard rummaging through the stereo or drawers with rustling and clanking noises. Suddenly, the stereo erupts with loud, aggressive rap music featuring a female vocalist shouting, "I want to take your fucking head off!" and "Help!" The woman observes him warily, her expression tense, as he turns with a weary, slightly defensive look. After a charged glance, he asks, "So, uh, you like music?" to which she replies, "Yeah. Most, I mean, I like rap." He then inquires, "So, uh, you got a boyfriend?" and she answers, "Not really." He presses, "Don't want me to be your boyfriend?" and she ambiguously responds, "I don't know. I'll tell ya," with the low, moody lighting underscoring the strained atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: In a brightly lit school hallway, a Young Black Male Student offers "Starbucks sandwiches" and suggests meeting at lunch to the Young Man on Bicycle, addressed as "Orlando." Orlando, wearing a navy blue jacket, opens a yellow locker and is joined by the Young Black Male Student, who animatedly discusses a girl with a "messed up spine" but who is "fine as hell" and interested in "sci-fi, comic book type stuff," calling her a "super fine sidekick" and Orlando "Cap." Orlando opens his locker to reveal a heart-shaped bead decoration. The scene shifts to a dimly lit office with a Man in Suit, identified as "Hanna," sitting at a desk and drinking from a mug. A Young Man with Blonde Hair, wearing a gray hoodie, defensively states, "This ain't my stuff... Obviously, there's actual books in it." A loud thud is heard, followed by the Young Man with Blonde Hair shouting, "Would you shut the fuck up, please?" The Man in Suit calmly interjects, "Hey, we don't talk like that in here," to which the Young Man with Blonde Hair defiantly replies, "I said please." Later, in a dimly lit office with an American flag and bookshelves, the bald Man in Suit, wearing a dark suit and striped tie, sits behind a cluttered wooden desk. He engages in a heated conversation with the Young Man on Bicycle, identified as Wayne, who enters demanding, "Where's all my stuff?" The Man in Suit sternly reprimands Wayne, telling him to leave and that he will talk to him later, but Wayne insists, "Go with us." The Man in Suit then addresses the Young Man with Blonde Hair, identified as Darren, telling him to sit down. Wayne, near the door, expresses frustration that "All my stuff from my locker is gone," specifically mentioning, "somebody took my nunchucks." The Man in Suit reprimands Wayne for bringing them to school, and Wayne argues, "I don't know where they are," complaining that "these kids, they steal everything," even recalling, "They stole Mrs. Healey's shoes a few weeks ago." The Man in Suit warns Wayne, "Wayne, Wayne, sit down, talk to me. I could have called truancy like five times by now and I haven't." Wayne leaves somberly. The Man in Suit, behind his desk with papers, a blue folder, a mug, a banana, and figurines, asks the Young Man on Bicycle about his father, noting their long relationship. The Young Man on Bicycle sarcastically accuses the Man in Suit of passing down a tradition of violence and beating him at his birthday party. The Man in Suit erupts, shouting "KNOCK IT OFF!" and slamming his hand on the desk, demanding he "CUT THAT SHIT OUT RIGHT NOW!" He then checks the hallway where the Young Man with Blonde Hair and a Third Young Man are visible. The Man in Suit explains that for every time the Young Man's dad beat him, he beat someone else twofold. The office, with a window showing bare trees, maintains a cold atmosphere. The Man in Suit, leaning forward intensely, states, "Now he was an asshole, no doubt. But he also couldn't stand for anybody getting away with doing something wrong. Does that sound like anybody else we know?" He offers a banana to Wayne, who peels and eats it. The Man in Suit advises Wayne to let proper people, like himself and the law, handle things. Wayne interjects, "Well, no one handled it for my dad." The Man in Suit acknowledges that Wayne's father "got screwed in court" and that Wayne has "every reason to be mad." When Wayne cuts him off, saying, "I don't care about her, okay?" regarding his mom, the Man in Suit cautions, "If you run around righting wrongs all your life, that'll be your life. And I know it's easy to follow in family footsteps, but you don't have to."

A young man in a gray hoodie and aviator goggles, intensely focused, races ahead of a semi-truck on a dark highway, while a young woman in a black helmet clings tightly behind him, her face etched with concern. Distorted, aggressive chants like "Don't let your son get you" are overlaid on the music, amplifying the tension. The scene cuts to a title card displaying 'WAYNE', introduced by a clear female voiceover stating, "This season on Wayne." An authoritative man in a suit declares, "I'm ready and committed to finding your daughter and to finding this son of a bitch." The man in the suit firmly refuses to take a student on a road trip, while another male voice warns, "You realize all of this is off the clock." The young man on the motorcycle declares, "We're going to Florida," prompting the young woman to exclaim in frustration, "I cannot believe I came here with you, and you don't even know where the hell we're going!" The montage continues, showing a gritty outdoor campsite, a man in a suit talking to the young man, and a young man carrying a TV. The young black male student, browsing vinyl records, exclaims, "Oh shit. It's Coffee Boy," followed by a loud "WHOA!" as the scene shifts to a garage with a gold muscle car. A menacing man in a red sleeveless shirt with gold chains challenges, "Who the fuck you think you is?" The sequence concludes with the motorcycle speeding away into the night, as the rock music swells, and the YouTube Originals logo appears.
